hook: The reason your cabinets stay messy is that nothing has a home.

scenes.visual_prompt: messy cabinet with stuff piled at the front and unknown void at back
scenes.voiceover: Your cabinets are messy because nothing has a home.

scenes.visual_prompt: person pulling everything out to reach back of cabinet
scenes.voiceover: The real problem is depth, not size.

scenes.visual_prompt: back of cabinet revealed full of forgotten items
scenes.voiceover: If you can't reach the back, the back is invisible.

scenes.visual_prompt: Simple Houseware chrome 2-tier sliding basket installed
scenes.voiceover: Sliding basket fixes the depth problem.

scenes.visual_prompt: front shelf slides forward revealing back shelf still in place
scenes.voiceover: Back row is always one motion away.
Comment LINK and I'll DM you the link.

caption:
The reason your cabinets stay messy is that nothing has a home.

Containers don't fix this. Bigger cabinets don't fix this. The problem is depth — when you can't reach the back without removing the front, the back becomes invisible.

A sliding basket organizer fixes the depth problem. Simple Houseware's chrome 2-tier model — each shelf pulls forward independently, so the back row is always one motion away.

Stopped buying duplicates of stuff I already owned. The cabinet became a system instead of a graveyard.

Comment LINK and I'll DM you the link.
